description:
Advances: docs (requirement 5 of the P1)

1. DELETE every test_helper.exs formatter-wiring snippet: docs/adoption.md:131-144 and :292-313, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/SKILL.md:147, references/adoption-phases.md:156-172 (incl. the escape-hatch line), references/task-templates.md:303-327, references/detection-matrix.md:152, priv/spec_init/agents/skills/spec-led-development/SKILL.md.eex daily-loop step 7. Replace with: 'Setup is one command: mix spec.cover.test. Never add SpecLedEx.Coverage.Formatter anywhere — it is inert unless the task arms it.' Phase-4 detection heuristic: artifact-exists / CI-runs-cover instead of grep-for-Formatter. 2. Fix false 'mix spec.check runs triangulation' claims (docs/adoption.md:25-27,137-139,305-313,356-361,423-434; docs/concepts.md:259-265; scaffolded SKILL.md.eex finding table lines 30-31,58-61) — name the real consumers spec.triangle + spec.review (Decision 1). 3. NEW docs/coverage.md linked from a README command table listing both coverage commands: one-command entry; coveralls coexistence + mix spec.cover.ingest; what the numbers mean (percent = ANY-test; self-verified = additionally an executed tagged test); --per-test contract (serialized required, async fails, --allow-async degrades, measured cost model, scoping knobs); prerequisites (tracer manifest); hygiene (.gitignore .spec/_coverage/ and cover/, generated_at staleness); full refusal-reason catalogue with fixes. 4. priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex: run mix spec.cover.test before mix spec.review. 5. 0.4.0 release notes lead with behavior breaks: test_helper wiring now no-ops loudly; spec.cover.test default changed (no serialization, envelope artifact, name kept per Decision 2); async during --per-test fails; legacy artifacts rejected (Decision 5).

## Orchestrator addenda (admission, epic @ 170c564)

All 8 code/spec tickets are merged — the docs must describe LANDED reality, verified against the tree, not the design doc. Ground truth: default 'mix spec.cover.test' = plain 'mix test --cover --export-coverage specled' + Aggregate.ingest to a v2 envelope (exit-code passthrough; non-zero + {:refused,...} sidecar on empty/refusal); 'mix spec.cover.ingest <path.coverdata>' = coveralls/CI escape hatch; '--per-test' = opt-in serialized lane on Snapshot native/classic engine (async hard-fail, --allow-async degrades); formatter inert unless task-armed (bare test_helper wiring = one stderr notice, no-op); legacy artifacts rejected with a message naming mix spec.cover.test (decision 5); task name unchanged (decision 2); consumers = spec.triangle + spec.review only, spec.check never runs triangulation (decision 1); recommend OTP >= 27.2 for native, no hard gate (decision 4).

HONEST-LANGUAGE RULE (binding, from the 155.8 cycle): docs must never claim 'exact' per-test attribution — use observed/race-bounded language consistent with the aggregate_first ADR, referencing specled_-cpw (ExUnit cast race) and the file-level proxy reality (specled_-jjq) where per-test precision is discussed. Percent = covered-by-ANY-test; self-verified = composite verdict, never a per-spec percent.

Confirmed file paths for deliverable 1 (audit line numbers may have drifted — grep for 'Coverage.Formatter' and 'formatters:' in each): docs/adoption.md, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/SKILL.md, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/references/adoption-phases.md, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/references/task-templates.md, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/references/detection-matrix.md, priv/spec_init/agents/skills/spec-led-development/SKILL.md.eex. Sweep the WHOLE repo for the test_helper wiring instruction, not just this list.

Release notes = a 0.4.0 (Unreleased) section in CHANGELOG.md leading with the behavior breaks; do NOT bump VERSION (the release PR flow owns that).

The doc-identifier lint gate applies: every command, flag, finding code, module/function name, and path you write must resolve against the tree — validate by execution (run the commands with --help / real fixtures) wherever possible.

LESSONS:
- append new code below existing api_boundary-bound functions (specled_-wp7, from 155.1)
- mix compile --force before spec.check seeds hashes for brand-new bound functions (from 155.6)
- reconcile-ALL-claim-surfaces obligations need repo-wide greps incl. @doc strings and test names; disclose survivors, never claim completion past them (from 155.8)

## Allowed touches

- docs/** (adoption.md, concepts.md, NEW docs/coverage.md)
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md
- skills/spec-led-bootstrap/** (SKILL.md + references/*)
- priv/spec_init/agents/skills/spec-led-development/SKILL.md.eex
- priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex
- .spec/specs/*.spec.md ONLY for gate-demanded co-change (docs subject bindings, if any)
- .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-regenerated)

## Out of Scope (files)

- lib/**, test/** (no code or test changes — docs-only ticket)
- VERSION
- .spec/decisions/** (ADRs are complete; do not amend)

## Out of Scope (intent)

- No behavior changes anywhere
- No new claims beyond landed reality; no 'exact' attribution language
- No VERSION bump

## Verification

- Zero instances of the SpecLedEx.Coverage.Formatter test_helper wiring instruction anywhere in the repo (grep proof); replaced with the one-command guidance + 'never add the formatter anywhere — it is inert unless the task arms it'
- Zero remaining false 'mix spec.check runs triangulation' claims in docs/skills/scaffolds (grep proof); real consumers (spec.triangle, spec.review Coverage tab) named
- NEW docs/coverage.md covers: one-command entry; coveralls coexistence + mix spec.cover.ingest; what the numbers mean (ANY-test percent; self-verified composite); --per-test contract (serialized required, async fails, --allow-async degrades, race-bounded attribution language, cost model); OTP posture; prerequisites (tracer manifest); artifact hygiene (.gitignore .spec/_coverage/ + cover/, generated_at staleness); full refusal-reason catalogue with fixes
- README command table lists both coverage commands, links docs/coverage.md
- spec_review.yml.eex runs mix spec.cover.test before mix spec.review
- CHANGELOG 0.4.0 section leads with behavior breaks (test_helper wiring no-ops loudly; default mode changed, name kept; async under --per-test fails; legacy artifacts rejected)
- Phase-4 detection heuristic updated (artifact-exists / CI-runs-cover, not grep-for-Formatter)
- Every documented command/flag/path validated by execution; doc-identifier lint green
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors, full mix spec.check, format all green
title: T9: Docs + onboarding sweep, docs/coverage.md, workflow template, release notes
